summary: Plan a guarded repair of contributor PR #94016. The hydrated artifact shows #94016 is an open, editable, focused PR for #94008 with passing checks, no review comments, and no security-sensitive signal. Merge and close actions are blocked by job policy, so Clownfish should build a repair artifact for the contributor branch and leave closeout for a later post-fix run.

actions.reason: Repair the editable contributor branch rather than merging or closing in this run; the executor must verify the exact stale-pending-task reproduction on a fresh checkout, make the narrow control-flow repair, run validation, and preserve contributor credit.
Issue #94008 is plausibly covered by canonical PR #94016, but closeout is not allowed before the fix is repaired and landed or opened by Clownfish.
A complete repair artifact is available and allowed; the deterministic executor should perform the branch repair and validation gates.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #94016 so Telegram channel recovery does not remain indefinitely deferred after a stop timeout leaves stale pending task state. Preserve the first-pass recovery return behavior, but ensure a later health-monitor pass that observes prior recovery intent clears stale pending task state and continues into normal channel start.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(gateway): resume channel after pending task recovery
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary

Repairs the Telegram channel timeout-recovery path from #94016 for #94008. When a channel stop timeout leaves stale pending task state, the first recovery pass can still defer startup, but a later monitor pass that sees recovery was already requested should clean up stale pending task state and continue into channel start.

## Credit

This repair preserves source PR credit for @sheyanmin in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94016 and issue attribution for #94008.

## Validation

- pnpm check:changed
- pnpm -s vitest run src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.test.ts
- /review
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @sheyanmin and PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94016.
Preserve issue attribution for #94008 by thomasthelen-kibeauftragter.
If a replacement PR is required instead of repairing the contributor branch, name #94016 as the source PR and carry contributor attribution in the PR body.
